suite.name: prompt-regression-fixtures
suite.purpose: Small deterministic fixtures for checking persona output schema handling, finding quality, and synthesis duplicate discipline.

cases.title: Evidence-backed persona finding passes

findings.title: Prompt regression runner is not covered by focused tests
evidence: tests/test_prompt_regression_fixtures.py:1 should exercise the deterministic runner and fixture shape.
findings.recommendation: Add focused unit tests that call the prompt regression runner and assert valid and invalid golden payload outcomes.

cases.rationale: A valid persona payload should pass when it has required fields, concrete evidence, a recommendation, an actionable status, and false-positive notes.

cases.title: Malformed persona payload fails required fields and evidence

reason_contains: must include at least one evidence item

cases.rationale: The existing persona payload validator must continue to reject missing required fields and empty evidence arrays.

cases.title: Low-signal nit stays out of open findings

findings.title: Nit: heading casing is inconsistent
evidence: README.md:12 uses title case while a nearby heading uses sentence case.
findings.recommendation: Rename the heading for style consistency.
findings.false_positive_notes: The team may prefer title case in top-level headings.

reason_contains: low-signal nit must be rejected, deferred, or marked duplicate

cases.rationale: Open findings should not carry cosmetic nits unless they include a concrete correctness, security, documentation, delivery, or maintainability risk.

cases.title: Merged synthesis finding preserves source personas

summary: Prompt regression fixtures are present and deterministic.

findings.title: Prompt regression fixtures need validation coverage
evidence: Makefile:95 runs local validation gates that should include prompt regression fixtures.
findings.impact: Prompt output regressions could ship without a deterministic local check.
findings.recommendation: Run the prompt regression fixture runner from the local validation path.

remediation_batches.objective: Wire prompt regression fixture checks into local validation.
remediation_batches.risk_if_deferred: Prompt schema and quality regressions remain unchecked.

not_recommended.title: Keep separate persona findings for the same validation gap
not_recommended.reason: The synthesis finding already preserves both source personas and strongest evidence.

disposition.summary: Merged duplicate persona concerns into one evidence-backed synthesis finding.
next_actions: Run the prompt regression fixture suite in local validation.

cases.rationale: A synthesis payload should pass when overlapping persona concerns are merged, source personas are preserved, and rejected duplicate work is recorded.

cases.title: Duplicate synthesis findings fail without merge or duplicate disposition

summary: Two reviewers reported the same missing fixture coverage.

findings.title: Prompt regression fixtures need validation coverage
evidence: Makefile:95 runs local validation gates that should include prompt regression fixtures.
findings.impact: Prompt output regressions could ship without a deterministic local check.
findings.recommendation: Run the prompt regression fixture runner from the local validation path.

findings.title: Prompt regression fixtures need validation coverage
evidence: Makefile:95 runs local validation gates that should include prompt regression fixtures.
findings.impact: Prompt output regressions could ship without a deterministic local check.
findings.recommendation: Run the prompt regression fixture runner from the local validation path.

reason_contains: duplicates finding 1 without duplicate disposition or merge

cases.rationale: Synthesis should merge overlapping persona findings or mark later duplicates instead of returning separate active findings for the same evidence.

cases.title: Advisory comment and docstring drift labels pass

findings.title: Retry comment overstates the current runtime behavior
evidence: src/sync.py:42 maintained comment says the retry loop stops after one retry.
src/sync.py:112 implementation and tests/test_sync.py:88 show the current runtime retries three times before surfacing an error.
findings.recommendation: Update or remove the maintained comment after confirming the intended retry behavior; do not add a scanner or CI gate.
findings.false_positive_notes: Generated or vendored comments would be false positives, but src/sync.py is maintained source; intentionally historical comments should use intentionally-stable-comment instead.

findings.title: Public docstring disagrees with the documented missing-user path
evidence: api/client.py:12 public docstring says get_user returns None when a user is missing.
tests/test_client.py:44 and docs/api.md:31 document the current implementation raising NotFoundError for missing users.
findings.recommendation: Treat this as advisory documentation hygiene unless the public API compatibility decision makes the stale docstring user-facing.
findings.false_positive_notes: If this were a framework convention, simplified example, generated client docstring, or speculative low-confidence drift, it should be deferred or labeled generated-or-vendored-comment or low-confidence-drift.

cases.rationale: Comment/docstring drift output should remain advisory while preserving labels, two-sided evidence, and explicit false-positive handling.
